Petition To Vacate Order Instruction Fidiciary Re: Asset Purchase Agreement And Purchase Money Note
Off Calendar Jan-15-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randall Schmidt for Ruth Pollack. Philip Shea appeared by telephone in Propria Person.

The Court stated that the petition has defective notice and that it appears to be a motion for reconsideration of a prior court order. Mr. Shea requested a continuance stating that he did not have the opportunity to respond to the opposition, and argued that he had a second valuation of the radio station done by Holt Media Group and that they valued the asset at $900,000.00 at the least, that the valuation the Estate has provided is flawed, that a similar business sold for $1,227,000.00 (Ohana Sale) and that Ex Line's valuation should be dismissed. Mr. Schmidt stated that he objects to a continuance, that the time to raise the issue of the appraisal and reconsideration of the order has long passed and that there was no due diligence by Mr. Shea. In addition, Mr. Schmidt argued that no one else was stepping forward with a better offer, that the Estate is in an abatement situation, that the time to file a Creditor's Claim has also long passed and addressed the issue of improper notice. Furthermore, Mr. Schmidt stated that AIM was not current on their monthly payment because they were waiting for this issue to be resolved, addressed the issues raised by the Court with regard to what would happen in the event AIM were to default, the radio stations being sold prior to the Decedent's death and stated that they have not worked out the mechanics of how distribution will be made. The Court denied Mr. Shea's request for a continuance and the Subsequent Petition to Vacate Order Instructing Fiduciary Re: Asset Purchase Agreement, etc, stated that arguments presented by Mr. Shea have been previously voiced on numerous occasions, that there are procedural defects and that the petition does not present grounds to reconsider the order filed on February 16, 2010. Status of resuming payment of Estate's expenses from recent $100,000.00 payment for Alaska Integrated Media (AIM) was continued to February 19, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to address the issue of Attorney's fees and expenses out of the cash currently on hand. Mr. Schmidt is to prepare an order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Linda Forster, CSR # 13286, Phone # (925) 548-1420. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Benton.
